strengths: Strong BLUF lead that delivers three concrete reveals in one sentence; no clickbait.
Quotes are faithful to the transcript and accurately paraphrased; the Cage Mount Rushmore, GoT death, Grogu, and NYC Mexican food claims all check out.
Excellent scannable structure: 7 H2s, most phrased as reader-benefit questions, plus Key Takeaways and bolded lead.
6 PAA-style FAQs that are specific and answer-rich, not filler.
Internal links to The Last of Us and The Mandalorian pages, plus 4 external authoritative sources.
Information gain via framing ('story structure' of the interview, fanboy ruined-viewing angle) rather than dry recap.
weaknesses: Headline keyword 'pedro pascal hot ones' is split awkwardly ('Pedro Pascal on Hot Ones'); primary keyword could be tighter and the favorite-Cage-movies angle is narrower than the multi-reveal lead promises.

The Mountain actor is named Hafþór Júlíus Björnsson — not in the transcript or grounding facts; this is an inferred addition that risks an accuracy ding (plausible but unsourced in provided material).
Meta/dek is solid but does not front-load the exact primary keyword string.
Some subheads cluster two near-identical 'what bothers him watching movies' themes; slight redundancy.
Could use one list or table (e.g., the Cage Mount Rushmore as a bulleted list) to boost scannability beyond prose.
Voice is competent but occasionally leans on summary phrasing ('demonstrating his unwavering loyalty') that softens punch.
